title: Emma's Big Day at Grandma's
synopsis: Emma feels scared about staying at Grandma's house for the first time, but with love and patience, she discovers that new places can become special too when filled with caring people.

pages.text: Emma hugged her teddy bear tight. Today she was going to stay at Grandma Rosa's house. Emma felt scared. She had never slept anywhere but her own bed before.

illustration.description: A young girl with curly dark hair wearing a yellow dress, clutching a brown teddy bear close to her chest. Her eyes are wide with worry and her mouth turned down slightly. She's standing in a bright living room with a red couch behind her. The style is warm cartoon art with soft textures resembling construction paper collage.
illustration.alt: Emma looking worried while holding her teddy bear

pages.text: Mama knelt down next to Emma. 'I know you feel scared, mi amor,' Mama said softly. 'It's okay to feel scared about new things. Grandma Rosa loves you so much.'

illustration.description: A caring mother with long black hair in a blue top kneeling beside Emma, gently touching her shoulder. Emma still looks uncertain but less tense. The background shows a sunny yellow wall with family photos. The illustration uses warm, comforting colors with rounded shapes and gentle expressions.
illustration.alt: Mama comforting Emma with gentle words

pages.text: Grandma Rosa opened her arms wide. 'Hola, Emma!' she said with a big smile. Emma still felt a little scared, but Grandma's warm hug made her feel a tiny bit better.

illustration.description: An elderly woman with silver hair in a purple cardigan and floral dress, arms open wide with a joyful expression. Emma is approaching hesitantly but with curiosity. The setting is a cozy entryway with bright tiles and potted plants. The art style emphasizes warmth through golden lighting and soft, welcoming shapes.
illustration.alt: Grandma Rosa welcoming Emma with open arms

pages.text: Grandma Rosa showed Emma her special room. There were colorful blankets and a night light that looked like stars. 'This is just for you, mija,' Grandma said. Emma started to feel happy.

illustration.description: A cozy bedroom with a small bed covered in bright rainbow blankets and pillows. A star-shaped night light glows softly on the nightstand. Emma's eyes are now bright with wonder as she explores, while Grandma Rosa watches proudly. The room has warm orange walls with children's drawings taped up.
illustration.alt: Emma discovering her special room at Grandma's house

pages.text: They made cookies together. Emma mixed the dough and Grandma helped her make heart shapes. Emma felt so happy! She wasn't scared anymore. She felt brave and loved.

illustration.description: Emma and Grandma Rosa at a kitchen counter covered in flour, both smiling broadly while making heart-shaped cookies. Emma is standing on a bright red step stool, flour on her cheeks, looking delighted. The kitchen has cheerful green cabinets and warm lighting. Cookie cutters and mixing bowls are scattered around in a joyful mess.
illustration.alt: Emma and Grandma Rosa happily baking cookies together

pages.text: At bedtime, Emma hugged Grandma Rosa tight. 'I love you, Grandma,' she whispered. 'I'm not scared anymore. I feel happy here with you.' And Emma had the sweetest dreams.

illustration.description: Emma in cozy pajamas with star patterns, giving Grandma Rosa a big hug beside the bed. Both have peaceful, loving expressions. The star night light casts a gentle glow around the room. Emma's teddy bear sits on the pillow. The scene is rendered in soft blues and purples with golden highlights creating a serene bedtime atmosphere.
illustration.alt: Emma hugging Grandma Rosa goodnight, feeling safe and loved

metadata.illustrationStyle: Bright, cheerful cartoon style with expressive faces. Bold primary colors. Rounded, friendly shapes. Slightly textured backgrounds like construction paper.

questions.question: How did Emma feel at the beginning of the story?
questions.explanation: Emma felt scared because she had never slept anywhere but her own bed before.

questions.question: What made Emma start to feel better?
questions.explanation: Grandma Rosa's warm hug made Emma feel a tiny bit better when she was still scared.

questions.question: What did Emma and Grandma Rosa do together?
questions.explanation: Emma and Grandma Rosa made heart-shaped cookies together, which made Emma feel happy.

questions.question: How did Emma feel at the end of the story?
questions.explanation: By the end, Emma felt happy and loved. She wasn't scared anymore and felt brave.

pages.title: Fill in the Feelings
pages.text: Emma felt _____ about staying at Grandma's house. But Grandma's hug made her feel _____. When they made cookies, Emma felt very _____!

pages.title: Emma's Special Day
pages.text: Grandma Rosa showed Emma her special _____. They made _____ together in the kitchen. Emma wasn't _____ anymore!
